text: They opened the envelope expecting another bill and instead a check fell out, with their name on it, for an amount that made them sit down on the kitchen floor and read the letter again from the beginning
The pregnancy test had two lines and she held it under the bathroom light and then under the window light and then took another one and it also had two lines

He turned the key in the lock of the house he had just bought and the previous owners had left every room painted a different vivid color, floor to ceiling, none of which had appeared in the listing photos
The oncologist said the word remission and then kept talking but the patient did not hear the next three sentences because the room had gone very bright and very quiet
She pulled the old coat from the attic box and a folded fifty-dollar bill fell from the pocket along with a movie ticket stub from 1987 and a note in her dead mother's handwriting
The garden had been neglected all winter and when they pushed open the back gate in April there were tulips everywhere, hundreds of them, in colors they had never planted
He answered the phone expecting a telemarketer and instead heard the voice of his college roommate, who he had not spoken to in nineteen years, calling from a city he had never heard of
The final grade posted online was an A and they refreshed the page four times and then checked that they were logged into the correct student account
When the movers tilted the old bookcase away from the wall there was a door behind it, painted shut, with a glass knob, leading to a narrow room with a window overlooking the garden
The rescue dog they had been told was timid walked directly to the child in the wheelchair and placed its head in her lap and did not move for twenty minutes

She bit into the plain-looking pastry from the street vendor and the filling was something she had never tasted before, warm and bright and layered, and she stood completely still on the sidewalk with her eyes closed
The DNA test results loaded on the screen and there was a half-sibling listed, someone with a name they had never encountered, living forty miles away
He opened the storage unit his late uncle had left him expecting boxes of old magazines and instead found a restored 1967 motorcycle under a canvas tarp, keys hanging from the handlebar

The email from the literary journal was a single paragraph and the word accepted was in the first sentence and they read it standing in the grocery store checkout line and forgot to put their items on the belt
When the power came back on after the three-day outage, every clock in the house was flashing except the old wind-up on the mantel, which had kept perfect time through the entire blackout
They pulled into the driveway after the long shift and every window in the house was lit and there were cars lining the street and someone had hung a banner across the garage that said welcome home
The bird that had been visiting the feeder every morning for two years landed on their outstretched hand for the first time, its weight almost nothing, its feet surprisingly warm
She opened the old piano bench to look for sheet music and found a stack of letters tied with kitchen string, all addressed to her grandmother, all from someone whose name she did not recognize
The contractor pulled up the worn carpet and beneath it was hardwood flooring in near-perfect condition, dark oak with an inlaid border that must have taken someone weeks to design
His daughter, who was four and had never been in the ocean, walked straight into the waves without stopping and turned around laughing with her arms wide open as if she had done this a thousand times
The rejection letter from the company was followed three days later by a phone call from the CEO personally, offering a different position that had not been listed
They weighed the pumpkin from the backyard patch on the bathroom scale and it read forty-seven pounds and they weighed it again because the seed packet had said the variety maxed out at twelve
The security camera footage showed a fox walking calmly through the open back door at three in the morning, eating from the cat's bowl, and leaving the same way without disturbing anything
She found her own name in the acknowledgments section of a book written by someone she had met only once, at a conference, seven years ago, for a conversation she barely remembered having
The mechanic called and said the engine was fine, that the sound had been a loose heat shield, a ten-dollar fix, and they had already budgeted three thousand for a replacement
When the ice thawed on the pond in spring there was a bicycle standing upright on the bottom, visible through the clear water, as though someone had ridden it in and parked it there
The toddler who had not yet spoken a full sentence looked at the painting in the museum and said that one is sad and then went back to eating crackers from a bag
He checked the old savings account his parents had opened when he was born, expecting a few hundred dollars, and the balance had five digits because of a stock transfer he had never been told about
The package had no return address and inside was a ceramic bowl she had made in a college art class twenty years ago, wrapped carefully in newspaper from a city she had never visited
They turned on the radio in the rented cabin and the first song that played was the one from their wedding, which had never been a hit and was rarely played anywhere

The old woman at the bus stop answered their small-talk question in fluent Japanese, then switched to French, then laughed and explained she had been a translator for the United Nations for thirty years
When the tide went out farther than they had ever seen it go, there were the remains of a stone wall on the ocean floor, running perfectly straight for a hundred yards, covered in barnacles
The barista handed them the wrong order and they took a sip before realizing, and whatever the drink was it was better than anything they had ever ordered on purpose

They opened the time capsule they had buried as a child and inside, along with the baseball card and the letter, was a twenty-dollar bill that their eight-year-old self had apparently decided future-them would need
The surgeon came out of the operating room four hours ahead of schedule, still in scrubs, and said it went perfectly and the whole waiting room went silent because no one had expected that word
She clicked on the street-view image of her childhood home and the tree she had planted in third grade was enormous now, taller than the house, its branches shading the entire front yard
The cat brought something in through the cat flap and set it gently on the rug and it was not a mouse or a bird but a single perfect strawberry from the neighbor's garden
They were cleaning out the attic and found a painting behind the insulation that was signed and dated 1923 and depicted the exact view from the window they were standing next to
The vote count was read aloud and their name had won unanimously, every single member, and the room was looking at them and they had not prepared any remarks because they had expected to lose

He cracked open the geode with a hammer in the garage and inside was not the typical quartz but something deep violet and crystalline that caught the overhead light and scattered it across the ceiling
The basement wall they knocked through during renovation opened into a second room that was not on any blueprint, with a window that looked out onto a garden no one in the family had planted
